texto: When I went into the business, I sat down and figured that I was indeed one of fortune's children. Just think. There were 20 million buffalo, each worth at least $3 -- $60 million. At the very outside, cartridges cost 25 cents each, so every time I fired one I got my investment back twelve times over. I could kill a hundred a day.... That would be $6,000 a month -- or three times what was paid, it seems to me, the President of the United States. Was I not lucky that I discovered this quick and easy way to fortune? I thought I was.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Frank Mayer que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “When I went into the business, I sat down and figured that I was indeed one of fortune's children…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, capitalism, hunting, com foco em hunting e old-west.
